verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1) is primarily a cytosolic chaperone with no TM domain or classical secretory signal. However, extensive literature documents its translocation to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane specifically in tumor and heat-stressed cells, via direct embedding driven by its own binding to phosphatidylserine, Gb3, and phosphoinositides. Surface presence is confirmed by cell-surface biotinylation and flow cytometry with the cmHsp70.1 mAb (detecting a C-terminal extracellular epitope on viable cells). Multiple therapeutic modalities (antibodies, ADCs, NK-ADCC, radioligands, nanoparticles) actively target membrane-HSPA1A on tumor cells. Surface localization is cell-state-induced (stress/cancer) rather than constitutive in normal cells.
